assumptions: The dividend receivable is a foreign-currency-denominated intragroup monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies, so the related foreign exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Any hedge relationship considered would designate only the foreign currency risk component and would meet the formal designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup monetary receivable is a genuine candidate hedged item in consolidation for its foreign currency risk under the intragroup monetary-item exception.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 provides cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct route for foreign-currency-driven variability in the cash flows of a recognised asset or liability.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Where the exposure is documented at the foreign-operation level rather than on the receivable itself, hedge accounting can be applied as a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation.
